title: Darkside (space station)

{{Quote|On Darkside, respect has to be earned.|[[Dina Mertz]], chief tech officer of Darkside|<ref name="MW" />}}
'''Darkside''' was a space station built on an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] in the [[Cularin asteroid belt]]. It was used as a repair station by the criminal [[organization]] of the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]]. The station had a bad reputation among the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], and many workers filed for transfers from the station on psychiatric grounds, which led to the station's name. Other possible origin of the name was because the station was always in the shadow of three other bigger asteroids.<ref name="MW" />

The crewers of Darkside were a surly, quiet lot, focused on their jobs. The boss of the station was the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Vin Tal'benes]], who commonly complained because he was in need of technical qualified staff but received mostly the remains of personnel not required elsewhere. Personnel rotation was too high that Tal'benes was commonly training newcomers.<ref name="MW" />

In the dark, not-often-used back corridors of Darkside lurked strange [[Oblee|shadow lurkers]], which drained the electrical charges from [[Power cell/Legends|power cells]] on such items as [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and [[Glowrod/Legends|glowrods]]. The crewers of the station were largely ignorant of these creatures, though it seemed to be the ignorance of refusing to acknowledge their existence. Another mystery of the back corridors of Darkside was a green room which could move to different points on the station with a flash of red light.<ref name="MW" />

Payday on Darkside was sometimes a special occasion as [[Nirama]] would send shipments of supplies on that day. The good food and good drink were a cause to celebrate, and once he even sent a band. The [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] on Darkside was called simply [[The Bar]]. Darkside had been previously controlled by Nirama's predecessor [[Riboga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], but Riboga's policies about Darkside are unknown.<ref name="MW" />

[[The Cell]], a faction of the Organization wanting to oust Nirama, had a presence on Darkside in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], but they were locally considered harmless urchins. Nevertheless, a raid by Nirama's forces took out all of the local leadership (except for [[Ahlya]], [[Dor Balse]], [[Kruss Gaal]], [[Jarrilyn]], [[Jasper]] and [[Michael (Darkside)|Michael]], all of them low-level members of the Cell). Not knowing how thoroughly he had upset the Cell there, Nirama sent a group of spies to Darkside. The spies were able to find the few remaining members of the Cell there and turned them over to Nirama.<ref name="MW" />

Although the spies left Darkside and the Cell leaders were captured at the same time, most of the people in Darkside did not connect both facts, as people came and went all the time. Local security chief [[Nala Noos]] understood the meaning but, as she was loyal to Nirama, decided not to tell anyone about Nirama's undercover agents. Noos was re-assigned to [[Rothe Gate]] soon afterwards.<ref name="UW">{{LivingForce|Between the Worlds: UnderWorld}}</ref>
